steps.title: Identify what y represents
steps.content: The variable $y$ represents the total amount of fat (in pounds) that the polar bear ate during the entire 5-day period described in the problem.

steps.title: Extract the given information and write the equation
steps.content: The problem states: In 5 days, a polar bear ate 22.0 pounds of fat. Since \(y\) is defined as the total amount eaten in these 5 days, we directly have \(y = 22\).

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '\(y = 22 + 5\)': Adding the number of days to the pounds has no meaning here — the total is 22 pounds, not 27. ✗
  • Choice B — '\(y = 22\)': The polar bear ate exactly 22.0 pounds total over the 5 days — \(y\) equals that constant. ✓
  • Choice C — '\(y = \frac{22}{5}\)': This gives the average per day (4.4 lb/day), but \(y\) represents the total over all 5 days, not the daily rate. ✗
  • Choice D — '\(y = 5\)': 5 is the number of days, not the amount of fat eaten. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
